# Combined Quality Checklist: Weather Overlay Explorer

**Purpose**: Validate the quality of UX, data, security, API, and performance requirements across the UC-09 specification, plan, data model, and API contract before implementation review.
**Created**: 2026-03-13
**Feature**: [spec.md](/root/311-forecast-system/specs/009-add-weather-overlay/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the manager-visible overlay controls fully specified for enable, disable, and single-measure selection states? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK002 Are requirements documented for all supported weather measures, including whether any measure-specific units, labels, or interpretation rules differ? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005, Contract §WeatherOverlayResponse]
- [x] CHK003 Are the data requirements complete for how geography and time-range inputs map from the forecast explorer into the overlay request model? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, Data Model §WeatherOverlaySelection]
- [x] CHK004 Are observability requirements defined for the full overlay lifecycle, including retrieval, alignment, supersession, successful rendering, and render failure? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011, Plan §Phase 0 Research Summary, Data Model §WeatherOverlayRenderEvent]
- [x] CHK005 Does the contract documentation specify all non-visible terminal states needed by the requirements, including whether `disabled` and `render_failed` are intentionally represented outside the `GET` response? [Gap, Spec §FR-008-FR-013, Data Model §OverlayDisplayState, Contract GET /api/v1/forecast-explorer/weather-overlay]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is "preserves the readability of all visible layers" quantified with objective UX criteria that reviewers can interpret consistently? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004]
- [x] CHK007 Is "supported geography" defined precisely enough to distinguish accepted matches from rejected geography requests under the approved alignment rules? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §FR-003, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK008 Is "supported selection" in the 5-second performance target bounded clearly enough to exclude invalid, unavailable, or superseded requests? [Clarity, Spec §SC-001, Plan §Performance Goals]
- [x] CHK009 Are "clear status message" requirements specific enough to determine whether different failure categories need distinct user-facing wording? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, Data Model §OverlayDisplayState]
- [x] CHK010 Is the distinction between `unavailable`, `unalignable`, `superseded`, and `render_failed` defined consistently enough for authors to know when each state applies? [Clarity, Data Model §OverlayDisplayState, Contract §WeatherOverlayResponse]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Are the overlay state definitions consistent between the spec, data model, and API contract, with no missing or conflicting statuses across documents? [Consistency, Spec §FR-007-FR-013, Data Model §OverlayDisplayState, Contract §WeatherOverlayResponse]
- [x] CHK012 Are the supported measures and their naming consistent across the spec, data model, use case, and contract (`temperature`, `snowfall`)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005-FR-006, Data Model §WeatherOverlaySelection, Contract query `weatherMeasure`]
- [x] CHK013 Do the security expectations stay consistent between the plan’s authenticated dashboard context and the API contract’s documented `401`/`403` outcomes? [Consistency, Plan §Summary, Plan §Constitution Check, Contract responses]
- [x] CHK014 Are the supersession rules consistent between the spec’s "latest selection only" language and the data model’s request and display-state transitions? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013, Data Model §WeatherOverlaySelection, Data Model §OverlayDisplayState]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Are all success criteria objectively measurable without needing implementation-specific interpretation? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001-SC-004]
- [x] CHK016 Do the success criteria cover UX, data safety, and responsiveness together rather than emphasizing only successful overlay display? [Coverage, Spec §SC-001-SC-004]
- [x] CHK017 Is there a measurable requirement for API or contract-level response behavior when overlay requests are unauthorized, forbidden, or invalid? [Gap, Contract responses, Plan §Constitution Check]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK018 Are primary, alternate, and exception-flow requirements complete for enable, disable, supersession, missing data, alignment failure, and render failure paths? [Coverage, Spec §User Stories 1-3, Spec §Edge Cases, UC-09-AT traceability]
- [x] CHK019 Are recovery requirements defined for how the overlay returns from a non-visible state to a visible state after the user changes to a valid selection? [Gap, Spec §User Story 3, Data Model §OverlayDisplayState]
- [x] CHK020 Are contract-quality requirements documented for both the retrieval endpoint and the render-event endpoint, including their relationship to the same overlay request identifier? [Coverage, Contract GET /api/v1/forecast-explorer/weather-overlay, Contract POST /render-events, Data Model §WeatherOverlayRenderEvent]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK021 Are boundary requirements specified for repeated rapid toggles and repeated filter changes, including whether duplicate requests or duplicate logs are acceptable? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-010-FR-013]
- [x] CHK022 Does the spec define what should happen when weather observations exist but measurement units or time granularity differ from the active explorer view? [Edge Case, Data Model §WeatherObservationSet, Spec §FR-003]
- [x] CHK023 Are requirements defined for empty but successful weather-provider responses versus explicit retrieval failures, so reviewers can distinguish missing-data behavior from service-error behavior? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2, Data Model §WeatherObservationSet]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK024 Are UX requirements specified for visual distinction, status visibility, and non-obscuring overlays in a way that can be reviewed objectively? [Non-Functional, Spec §FR-004, Spec §SC-002]
- [x] CHK025 Are performance requirements defined for the most important request classes, including first load, filter-change refresh, and superseded-request cleanup? [Non-Functional, Spec §SC-001, Spec §SC-004, Plan §Performance Goals]
- [x] CHK026 Are security requirements complete for authenticated access, role assumptions, and protection against exposing raw third-party weather payloads? [Non-Functional, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Constraints, Plan §Constitution Check]
- [x] CHK027 Are API requirements complete for validation errors, status-code intent, and stable response shapes across success and explicit non-visible states? [Non-Functional, Contract responses, Contract §WeatherOverlayResponse]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK028 Are the assumptions about approved geography-alignment rules and Edmonton-area station selection documented precisely enough to avoid hidden planning decisions? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Implementation Steps 2-3]
- [x] CHK029 Are dependencies on the existing forecast explorer context and prior visualization feature documented well enough to show what UC-09 reuses versus what it defines? [Dependency, Plan §Summary, Data Model §Reused Context From Previous Specs]
- [x] CHK030 Is the assumption that no separate overlay activation store is needed validated against the required observability and traceability outcomes? [Assumption, Plan §Technical Context, Data Model §OverlayDisplayState]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK031 Does the current documentation resolve the duplicate `Overlay Display State` definition in the spec, or is a canonical single definition still needed? [Conflict, Spec §Key Entities]
- [x] CHK032 Is it unambiguous whether the contract should expose a `disabled` state, given that the data model includes it but the `GET` contract omits it? [Ambiguity, Data Model §OverlayDisplayState, Contract §WeatherOverlayResponse]
- [x] CHK033 Are terms such as "optional", "clear", "readability", and "latest selection" defined tightly enough to avoid reviewer disagreement during implementation planning? [Ambiguity, Spec §User Stories, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-013]

## Notes

- This checklist combines UX, data, security, API, and performance requirement-quality review into one PR-focused checklist.
- Items are intended to evaluate the written requirements and supporting design documents, not implementation behavior.
